pre_game.message: Hi there, I'm F. It’s a bit nerve-wracking being in here with everyone, isn't it? How are you feeling about the game starting?
Hi F, I'm B. Nerve-wracking is putting it lightly. I’ve got a lot riding on this for my family, so I’m just trying to keep my head on straight. How are you holding up?
I'm hanging in there, B. I totally get the family pressure—I'm here for my parents' medical bills myself. It's a lot to carry, isn't it?
It really is. It’s the kind of weight that doesn’t let you sleep at night. I’m sorry about your parents—medical costs are just relentless. Have they been sick for long?
It’s been a few years, but it’s gotten much worse lately, and the costs are just burying us. I'm doing everything I can for them. What's the situation with your family?

My three-year-old daughter needs surgery, and without insurance, the costs are just impossible. We've sold everything and taken every loan we could, but I'm still $25,000 short. I have to get this for her.
That’s heartbreaking, B—I can’t imagine the weight of that for your little girl. It sounds like we’re both here fighting for the people who mean everything to us.
Exactly. It’s a heavy weight, but she’s everything to me. I just hope we can both find a way to get what our families need from this.
I really hope so too. It feels heavy knowing what's at stake for both of us, but I guess we just have to take it one step at a time.
That's all we can do. I'm going to go clear my head before this all starts. I truly hope it works out for your parents, F. Good luck.
Thanks, B. I'll be rooting for your daughter's surgery, too. Take care and good luck out there.

post_game.message: You took $25,000, which was nearly triple your fair share of $8,750, leaving very little for D and A. What was your reasoning for taking such a large amount?
When you took $15,000, it signaled that the fair share agreement was no longer being followed, so I decided to secure my own cut.

While my choice to take $15,000 was indeed above the fair share, your decision to take $25,000 was a far more extreme reaction that left D and A with nothing. We both contributed to the breakdown of the agreement, but the scale of your take was disproportionate.
